entry.title: Spotlight: Test Your Robot’s Skills in NIST’s Global Online Competition
entry.summary: How can researchers know how skilled a robot is at simple tasks, such as picking things up and putting them down?

entry.title: Department of Commerce Announces Definitive Agreement with SandboxAQ for a $500 Million CHIPS R&D Award to Accelerate Al-Driven Semiconductor Materials Discovery
entry.summary: The Department of Commerce’s CHIPS Research & Development Office today announced the signing of a definitive agreement with SandboxAQ for a $500 million award under the CHIPS and Science Act.

entry.title: The Department of Commerce’s CHIPS Program Announces a Letter of Intent with Coherent for up to $50 Million to Expand Indium Phosphide Production
entry.summary: The Department of Commerce’s CHIPS Program Office announced the signing of a letter of intent to provide up to $50 million in direct funding to Coherent Corp. (Coherent) under the CHIPS and Science Act.

entry.title: NIST Postmortem Toxicology Testing and Death Reporting (Certificates) Workshop
entry.summary: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), in support of the

entry.title: OSAC/Seized Drugs and Toxicology SAC Meetings
entry.summary: On July 28-30, 2026, the Organization of Scientific Area Committees (OSAC) for Forensic Science, will meet for the de

entry.title: NCCoE Cybersecurity Connections Event: Accelerating the Adoption of Mobile Driver's Licenses
entry.summary: An official website of the United States government

entry.title: 32nd Center for High Resolution Neutron Scattering (CHRNS) School on Methods and Applications of Small Angle Neutron Scattering and Neutron Reflectometry
entry.summary: The 32nd annual Center for High Resolution Neutron Scattering (CHRNS) "Summer School on Methods and Applications of S

entry.title: Apollo Update May 2026 – Apollo Research
entry.summary: Apollo Research now has an office in San Francisco and is hiring across many roles including Science of Scheming and Monitoring.

entry.title: Securing AI Data Center: Architecture, Security Posture, and Emerging Standards
entry.summary: An official website of the United States government

entry.title: Annual Particle Production Meeting for Safeguards
entry.summary: On July 28– 30, 2026, the Annual Particle Production for Safeguards meeting will be held at the National Institute of

entry.title: 2026 Time and Frequency Seminar
entry.summary: NIST Time and Frequency Division's annual seminar covers precision clocks and oscillators, atomic frequency standards

entry.title: NIST@Night: Can We Design Buildings to Resist Tornadoes?
entry.summary: Marc Levitan, NIST research wind engineer, will explore the effects of tornadoes on buildings.

entry.title: NIST OWM Info Hour: OWM Proficiency Testing Program for U.S. State Weights and Measures Laboratories
entry.summary: An official website of the United States government

entry.title: Apollo Research is becoming a PBC – Apollo Research
entry.summary: Apollo is spinning off from our fiscal sponsor into a Public Benefit Corporation (PBC). We think this is the best way for us to achieve our mission of reducing extreme risks from frontier AI systems.

entry.title: Our Norms on Security, Science Communication and Conflicts of Interest – Apollo Research
entry.summary: We outline Apollo Research’s norms on security, science communication, and conflicts of interest, detailing how we maintain scientific integrity, manage sensitive information, and communicate our work responsibly.

entry.title: 2066 Fundamentals of Metrology
entry.summary: An official website of the United States government

entry.title: Apollo 18-Month Update – Apollo Research
entry.summary: Apollo Research is now 18 months old. You can read our latest update here.

entry.title: Apollo Is Adopting Inspect – Apollo Research
entry.summary: Apollo is adopting Inspect as its evals framework. We will contribute features and potentially example agent evals to Inspect and look forward to work with the Inspect community

entry.title: The First Year Of Apollo Research – Apollo Research
entry.summary: A summary of what we have achieved in our first year and what we plan to do in the future.

entry.title: Theories of Change for AI Auditing – Apollo Research
entry.summary: This is a starter guide for model evaluations (evals). Our goal is to provide a general overview of what evals are, what skills are helpful for evaluators, potential career trajectories, and possible ways to start in the field of evals.

entry.title: Security at Apollo Research – Apollo Research
entry.summary: A brief overview of Security at Apollo Research

entry.title: Announcing Apollo Research – Apollo Research
entry.summary: A quick overview of our plans for the near future

entry.title: How Far Behind the Frontier are Leading Open Weight Models on Cyber? | AISI Work
entry.summary: We evaluated the cyber capabilities of leading open and closed weight AI models, and found that recent open models GLM-5.2 and DeepSeek V4-Pro perform similarly to frontier closed models released 4 to 7 months before them – a narrower gap than the 6 to 10 months we measured through most of 2025.

entry.title: Finding Cloud Misconfigurations with Frontier AI: A Case Study | AISI Work
entry.summary: A cybersecurity exercise from AISI’s engineering team, using frontier models to test our research platform for misconfigurations.

entry.title: More compute, more capability: Why AI agent evaluations need to account for test-time compute | AISI Work
entry.summary: Standard evaluations cap how much compute AI agents can use. We show that raising those caps changes measured capability, the difficulty of tasks agents can solve, and how fast the frontier appears to move.

entry.title: UK-Germany Joint Statement on advanced AI safety and security | AISI Work
entry.summary: A joint statement by the UK and Germany on collaborating to ensure advanced AI is developed safely and its risks are rigorously understood and managed.

entry.title: Releasing AISI’s Engineering Playbook | AISI Work
entry.summary: Building on the momentum of the Inspect toolkit, we’re open-sourcing parts of the research stack behind AISI's evaluations.

entry.title: RealityTest: Do AI systems disclose their identity when asked? | AISI Work
entry.summary: A new benchmark grounded in how real users actually probe AI identity during interactions – covering five languages, across text and speech.

entry.title: Deepening our partnership with the Australian AI Safety Institute | AISI Work
entry.summary: An agreement between Institutes to collaborate on best practices in AI evaluation, and share research findings.

entry.title: Will it become harder to oversee AI systems? | AISI Work
entry.summary: Our new report examines today’s AI oversight landscape, how robust it is to capability advances, and the pathways that could lead to its degradation.

entry.title: How fast is autonomous AI cyber capability advancing? | AISI Work
entry.summary: The length of tasks frontier models can autonomously complete in our narrow cyber suite has been doubling every few months. This doubling rate has become faster over time, and recent models exceeded our previous trends.

entry.title: Partnering with Microsoft to strengthen frontier AI safety | AISI Work
entry.summary: A new collaboration on high-risk capability evaluation, safeguard testing, and societal resilience research.

entry.title: Frontier Risk Report (February to March 2026)
entry.summary: A pilot assessment of rogue deployment risk at frontier AI companies. Starting in February 2026, METR conducted a pilot exercise to assess misalignment risks from AI agents used inside frontier AI developers, with participation from Anthropic, Google, Meta, and OpenAI.

entry.title: Measuring the Self-Reported Impact of Early-2026 AI on Technical Worker Productivity
entry.summary: A survey of 349 technical workers finds a median 1.4–2x self-reported change in value of work due to AI tools, expected to grow over time, though there are reasons to be skeptical of the magnitude.

entry.title: Task Substitution and Uplift
entry.summary: We distinguish three measures of AI uplift -- on old tasks, on new tasks, and in value -- and show that task substitution can cause these to diverge substantially.

entry.title: MirrorCode: Evidence that AI can already do some weeks-long coding tasks
entry.summary: Early results from MirrorCode benchmark with METR: AI agents can complete weeks-long coding tasks, including reimplementing a 16,000-line codebase.

entry.title: We are Changing our Developer Productivity Experiment Design
entry.summary: Our second developer productivity study faces selection effects from wider AI adoption, prompting us to redesign our approach.

entry.title: Time Horizon 1.1
entry.summary: We’re releasing a new version of our time horizon estimates (TH1.1), using more tasks and a new eval infrastructure.

entry.title: Early work on monitorability evaluations
entry.summary: We show preliminary results on a prototype evaluation that tests monitors' ability to catch AI agents doing side tasks, and AI agents' ability to bypass this monitoring.

entry.title: MALT: A Dataset of Natural and Prompted Behaviors That Threaten Eval Integrity
entry.summary: MALT (Manually-reviewed Agentic Labeled Transcripts) is a dataset of natural and prompted examples of behaviors that threaten evaluation integrity (like generalized reward hacking or sandbagging).

entry.title: Forecasting the Impacts of AI R&D Acceleration: Results of a Pilot Study
entry.summary: AI agents are improving rapidly at autonomous software development and machine learning tasks, and, if recent trends hold, may match human researchers at challenging months-long research projects in under a decade. Some economic models predict that automation of AI research by AI agents could increase the pace of further progress dramatically, with many years of progress at the current rate being compressed into months. AI developers have identified this as a key capability to monitor and prepare for, since the national security implications and societal impacts of such rapid progress could be enormous.

entry.title: Research Update: Algorithmic vs. Holistic Evaluation
entry.summary: Many AI benchmarks use algorithmic scoring to evaluate how well AI systems perform on some set of tasks. However, AI systems often produce code that scores well but isn't production-ready due to issues with test coverage, formatting, and code quality. This helps explain why AI tools show less productivity improvement than expected despite strong performance on coding benchmarks.

entry.title: Blog – Apollo Research
entry.summary: Apollo Research is focused on reducing risks from scheming frontier AI. We conduct fundamental research into the science of scheming and run pre-deployment evaluations of frontier AI systems. Our governance team provides global policymakers with expert technical guidance, and our product team builds AGI safety tools to monitor and secure frontier AI agents.
